design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect scattered Chirps (small birds), escort them in a chain to the exit while avoiding/defeating enemies with throwable items
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing a growing chain of dependent NPCs while evading threats; chain breaks on enemy contact, scattering progress
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Protective caretaker shepherding vulnerable creatures to safety through an urban obstacle course
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game explicitly centers on 'protecting the children' emotion rather than pure action; Chirps are followers with agency (some behave unpredictably when touched), not passive collectibles. Single-screen wraparound design keeps all action visible. Designed as a response to Namco's Mappy but with distinct protective caretaker identity

difficulty.checkpointing: Single-screen stages with wraparound; no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game loops infinitely on increased difficulty after 48 stages completed; no difficulty select documented. Throwable items provide tactical options for enemy avoidance/defeat

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential stage progression through 48 total stages; bonus rounds interspersed (catch falling Chirps in net)
replayability_hooks: Infinite loop mode with escalating difficulty after stage 48
Bonus points for collecting multiple Chirps in single chain (score incentive)
Variable Chirp behavior (sunglasses-wearing 'bad' Chirps act unpredictably)

world_building_techniques: Urban apartment building setting (inspired by real location outside developer's office)
Visual metaphor of power lines as platforms (from Japanese variety show reference)
Character relationships implied through mechanics: Flicky as protector/friend to Chirps, not parent

danger_signals: Enemy proximity triggering Chirp chain dispersal
Sunglasses-wearing Chirps indicating unpredictable behavior when threatened
environmental_storytelling: Apartment building architecture conveying urban domestic space
Throwable items (telephones, flower pots, cups, bottles) grounding action in realistic urban environment

sources_used: Wikipedia - Flicky article (gameplay section, development section)
